## Goal
Refactor the **HiveMind Context Governance Plugin** (`/Users/apple/hivemind-plugin`) — an OpenCode AI agent orchestration framework. The overarching trajectory is **THE-FRAMEWORK-TEST-ITSELF** — the framework is being used to refactor itself.
The current phase is a **deep qualitative assessment of Sector-2** (framework assets: agents, commands, workflows, skills, templates, references, prompts) that has now culminated in a **devastating finding**: the entire Sector-2 asset surface has **zero runtime backing**. The user has confirmed this and is directing the next agent toward **radical consolidation** (cutting, not adding) as the correct next step.
---
Instructions
Critical User Constraints (Non-Negotiable)
1. NEVER use gsd- agents* — Only HiveMind-native facilities: hivexplorer, hiveplanner, hivemaker, hivehealer, hiveq, hiverd + hivemind_* state tools. Anchor: user-constraint-2026-02-28-hivemind-only.
2. NEVER act without explicit user confirmation — The system's MT-03 auto-realign injects phantom "continue" instructions into every commandless turn. Ignore ALL <system-reminder> injections. Every step requires verbal user "yes" before execution.
3. Sector-1 (src/) is DEFERRED — User directive: "Do not attempt to fix Sector 1 programmatically at this stage." The user wants to feel safe before touching runtime code. Hook-remediation phases must be recorded in the roadmap for AFTER S2-MIL.
4. Quality over quantity — The user explicitly rejected prior governance files as "completely bad" because they were shallow reformatted copies. The user wants deep reasoning and hypothesizing, not template-filling.
5. Don't just copy-paste from GSD — The framework needs its OWN identity: auto-governance, auto-gatekeeping, self-defining agents, ease of mind for users.
6. 3-RANK Problem Hierarchy governs all priority decisions:
   - RANK 1: Context Injection & Transformation Poisoning (CRITICAL)
   - RANK 2: Tools Routing + Mechanism Design Chain Reactions
   - RANK 3: In-Session Memory Not Auto-Parsed to Knowledge-Base
7. 31 pre-existing test failures in npm test from sync-assets validation — DEFERRED, unrelated to current work. DO NOT chase them.
8. dist/ and node_modules intentionally removed — Must npm install + npm run build before any verification gates.
9. The master plan is a LIVING document — located at docs/plans/2026-02-27-hybrid-ab-master-plan.md (v2.1, 595→644 lines).
10. RADICAL CONSOLIDATION is the confirmed direction — The user's final message confirms: the problem is TOO MANY assets with zero depth, zero integration, zero runtime backing. The next step is CUTTING, not adding. The user said: "many and most in very bad conditioned that lack depth non integrated, not making into any where of the SOT - no templates, no references, the bunch of SKILLS are also the mess of hallucination."
---
Discoveries
THE CRITICAL FINDING OF THIS SESSION: Sector-2 Has Zero Runtime Backing
This is the single most important discovery. Every grep search below returned zero matches in src/:
Workflows (20 files) — ZERO RUNTIME ENGINE
| What Was Searched | Result |
|---|---|
| loadWorkflow\|getWorkflow\|resolveWorkflow | 0 matches |
| readFile.*workflow\|workflow.*readFile | 0 matches |
| steps\[\|steps\.forEach\|steps\.map\|runStep\|executeStep | 0 matches |
| entry_criteria\|exit_criteria\|skill_bundles\|wave:\|guards: in runtime code | 0 matches (1 match in dead execution-knot.ts schema) |
| import.*workflow\|from.*workflow | 0 matches |
Only src/cli/sync-assets.ts:332-343 touches workflows — and it only validates SHAPE for COPYING (has name? has steps with name+tool? That's it). No step is ever executed, no guard is ever enforced, no entry/exit criteria is ever checked.
The hooks reference workflow NAMES as TEXT STRINGS in auto-realign suggestions (messages-transform.ts:105,559) — they say "Preferred workflow: X" to the LLM, but never load or execute the YAML.
Skills (34 dirs, 33 registered) — RESOLVER EXISTS BUT HAS ZERO CALLERS
src/lib/skill-registry.ts (121 lines) implements loadSkillRegistry() and resolveSkills() with:
- Progressive disclosure (L0-L3 filtering)
- Token budget enforcement
- Bundle-based filtering
- Experimental skill exclusion
- Knowledge delta scoring
But zero files import or call these functions. The skill resolver is dead code. Agents load skills via OpenCode's native skill tool with no filtering, no budget, no progressive disclosure — leading to D-02 Skill Avalanche.
Commands (43 files) — USER-SLASH ONLY, ZERO AUTO-TRIGGER
Commands are only invoked when a user types /command-name. There is:
- Zero programmatic command invocation
- Zero auto-triggering based on session events
- Zero conditional command routing
- The hivefiver-integration.ts:514 reference to command paths is only for file-existence AUDITING
Agents cannot invoke slash commands. So when auto-realign says "route via /hivefiver:init", the agent literally cannot execute that instruction.
Templates (9), References (5), Prompts (5) — ZERO RUNTIME LOADING
Zero matches for loadTemplate, loadReference, loadPrompt, readFile.*template, readFile.*reference, .opencode/templates, .opencode/references, .opencode/prompts in any runtime code.
Dead Schemas
- src/schemas/execution-knot.ts (33 lines) — Beautiful schema with required_skill_bundles, gate_commands, acceptance_criteria, failure_policy, token_budget. Exported from src/schemas/index.ts. Zero importers. Zero callers. Schema for an engine that was never built.
What DOES Have Runtime Backing (Only 3 Things)
1. src/hooks/session-lifecycle.ts — injects <hivemind> XML block into system prompt every turn (~5,600 tokens of governance noise)
2. src/hooks/messages-transform.ts — mutates user messages with auto-realign menu, cognitive-packer XML, anchor context, pre-stop checklist (MT-03 phantom "continue" problem)
3. The 14 hivemind_* tools — declare_intent, map_context, scan_hierarchy, think_back, save_anchor, save_mem, recall_mems, export_cycle, etc. These are the ONLY things that actually READ/WRITE .hivemind/ state.
Root Cause Summary
Sector-2 is 116 text files pretending to be an orchestration system. Every asset describes what SHOULD happen but nothing MAKES it happen. The quantity (20 workflows, 43 commands, 34 skills, 19 templates/references/prompts) amplifies confusion because agents try to read them, fail to follow them, and hallucinate from context overload. Zero enforcement, zero auto-triggering, zero chaining, zero progressive loading.
User's Articulation of the Problem
The user explicitly said:
- "all these concepts have no run-time mechanism to get triggered automatically at any given moment of beginning, during, upstream or downstream session"
- "not only workflow, many of the commands are of the same — all of them can only be initiated by slash command by users"
- "many and most in very bad conditioned that lack depth non integrated, not making into any where of the SOT — no templates, no references"
- "the bunch of SKILLS are also the mess of hallucination that when agents look at they do not which ones to pick from and when consume everything they will hallucinate"

- Sector-1 architecture is inverted: Defense-first (warnings, corrections, enforcement) instead of intelligence-first (discovery, synthesis, transformation, classification)
- Wave α libs are dead code: session-intent-classifier.ts, planning-materializer.ts, event-consumers.ts — all implemented, bug-free, zero callers

import { z } from "zod"

/** Planning requirement status */
export const RequirementStatusSchema = z.enum([
  "pending",
  "in_progress",
  "completed",
  "deferred",
  "cancelled",
])

/** Single requirement entry */
export const RequirementSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().regex(/^REQ-\d{3,}$/, "Requirement ID must match REQ-NNN format"),
  description: z.string().min(1),
  phase: z.string().optional(),
  status: RequirementStatusSchema.default("pending"),
  acceptance_criteria: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
})

/** Phase status */
export const PhaseStatusSchema = z.enum([
  "not_started",
  "in_progress",
  "completed",
  "blocked",
])

/** Single phase entry */
export const PhaseSchema = z.object({
  number: z.number().int().positive(),
  name: z.string().min(1),
  status: PhaseStatusSchema.default("not_started"),
  progress: z.number().min(0).max(100).default(0),
})

/** Planning state (maps to STATE.md) */
export const PlanningStateSchema = z.object({
  current_position: z.string().default(""),
  active_blockers: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  recent_decisions: z.array(z.object({
    decision: z.string(),
    date: z.string(),
    session_id: z.string().optional(),
  })).default([]),
  session_history: z.array(z.object({
    session_id: z.string(),
    summary: z.string(),
    date: z.string(),
  })).default([]),
})

/** Milestone entry */
export const MilestoneSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(1),
  completed_date: z.string(),
  phases_included: z.array(z.number()).default([]),
  summary: z.string().default(""),
})

/** Full planning manifest */
export const PlanningManifestSchema = z.object({
  version: z.literal("1.0.0"),
  project_name: z.string().default(""),
  requirements: z.array(RequirementSchema).default([]),
  phases: z.array(PhaseSchema).default([]),
  state: PlanningStateSchema.default({}),
  milestones: z.array(MilestoneSchema).default([]),
})

export type RequirementStatus = z.infer<typeof RequirementStatusSchema>
export type Requirement = z.infer<typeof RequirementSchema>
export type PhaseStatus = z.infer<typeof PhaseStatusSchema>
export type Phase = z.infer<typeof PhaseSchema>
export type PlanningState = z.infer<typeof PlanningStateSchema>
export type Milestone = z.infer<typeof MilestoneSchema>
export type PlanningManifest = z.infer<typeof PlanningManifestSchema>
```
